title: Lucuma gardneri
intro: Lucuma gardneri, also known as the Peruvian lucuma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the Andean region of South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestive health, and possess antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lucuma gardneri include lucumol, a unique triterpenoid compound, as well as other phytochemicals such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lucuma gardneri is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to support heart health and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Lucuma gardneri is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to South America, specifically in Argentina and Chile. Lucuma is a genus of deciduous shrubs or small trees. The classification of Lucuma gardneri reflects its relationship to other species within the Sapindaceae family, which includes other plants such as the soapberry and the lychee. Lucuma gardneri is a member of the order Sapindales, which is a group of flowering plants known for their diverse range of species.
